items.title: I was in the room with Trump and heard the low thudding sound of gunfire
items.summary: The BBC's Gary O'Donoghue describes the moment he and others dived for cover as shots rang out at the venue.

items.title: Trump cancels US envoys' trip to Pakistan for talks on Iran war
items.summary: Iran had earlier said there were no plans for a direct meeting with a US delegation led by Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner.

items.title: Netanyahu orders army to 'vigorously attack' Hezbollah in Lebanon
items.summary: The Israeli PM's directive comes as six people were killed by strikes in Lebanon despite a ceasefire being extended by three weeks.

items.title: Armed groups launch coordinated attacks across Mali
items.summary: Witnesses report clashes in the centre and north, in what has been described as the largest jihadist attack in years.

items.title: Mexico says US agents killed in crash weren't permitted to operate there
items.summary: The two Americans who reportedly worked for the CIA died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a drug lab.

items.title: Palestinians in West Bank and some in Gaza vote in local elections
items.summary: Local elections have been held in the occupied West Bank and in one Gazan city, though Hamas and other groups are not taking part.

items.title: Rights groups critical as Venezuela prisoner release scheme 'coming to an end'
items.summary: More than 500 political prisoners are thought to still be in jail, despite the releases since the amnesty law was brought in.

items.title: Orbán steps down from Hungarian parliament after landslide defeat
items.summary: The outgoing prime minister will not take up his seat after leading his party back into opposition.

items.title: Thirty-one sloths die in Florida before opening of attraction
items.summary: Many died due to conditions at a warehouse they'd been shipped to, while others arrived dead, authorities say.

items.title: Seven dead in major Russian attack on Ukraine
items.summary: The city of Dnipro was hardest hit, with officials saying four died in a strike on a residential building.

items.title: Thousands at risk after multi-million dollar Everest flood warning system left to rust
items.summary: The flood warning system at Imja glacial lake has not been maintained since 2016, fearful locals tell BBC.

items.title: The viral manifesto of 'anti-woke' tech boss with NHS and defence contracts
items.summary: The boss of the controversial tech company with UK government contracts published a 22-point plan on the future of the West.

items.title: US to allow firing squads, gas and electrocution for federal executions
items.summary: In a memo, the Department of Justice says the move will "strengthen" the death penalty and deter crime.

items.title: Nato says 'no provision' to expel members after report US could seek to suspend Spain
items.summary: An internal Pentagon email reportedly outlines options to punish allies over a perceived lack of support for Iran war.

items.title: US justice department drops probe into Fed chairman Jerome Powell
items.summary: President Donald Trump had accused Powell of improper cost overruns in renovating the Fed's building.

items.title: China car giant BYD says it can thrive without US
items.summary: With the price of fuel rising China's BYD says it is positioning itself to benefit from the global shift away from fossil fuels.

items.title: Key suspect in notorious Tadamon massacre during Syria civil war arrested
items.summary: Amjad Youssef was wanted over the mass killing of blindfolded civilians in April 2013 in the district of Damascus.

items.title: Israeli police investigate after officers 'cut Palestinian flag' from skullcap
items.summary: The British Israeli man says he was detained after someone took offence at his kippah embroidered with Israeli and Palestinian flags.

items.title: Pirates hijack oil tanker off the coast of Somalia
items.summary: Piracy, once notorious in this stretch of the Indian Ocean, has made a comeback in recent years.

items.title: Mandelson under formal investigation by EU's anti-fraud office
items.summary: The EU's anti-fraud office confirms it has "sufficient information" to launch  an investigation, but does not say if it involves fraud.

items.title: 'We cried together': Trump's deportation drive forces tough decisions for couples
items.summary: The spouses of illegal immigrants facing deportation must choose between separation or moving to a new country.

items.title: Meet the men calling out 'masculinity' trends
items.summary: Influencers promoting looksmaxxing and narrow male beauty standards are being called out by a new wave of men on social media.

items.title: A nation built on pan-African principles faces questions about racism
items.summary: Several black Zambians tell the BBC they sometimes feel like they are second-class citizens in their own country.

items.title: Katya Adler: Europe's Nato allies push back at reported US threat to Spain
items.summary: Souring relations between Europe and the US have reared their Medusa-like head again, writes the BBC's Europe editor.

items.title: The Kashmir town trying to win back tourists after a deadly attack
items.summary: A year after the Pahalgam attack, tourism has slowed in Indian-administered Kashmir, hitting lives and livelihoods.

items.title: Behind Nigeria's murky coup plot - the money, the prayers and a Nollywood arrest
items.summary: What court papers and sources close to the investigation have told the BBC about the scope of the plan.

items.title: Who is making decisions in Iran?
items.summary: Iran's new supreme leader is supposed to have the final say, but in practice the picture is far murkier.

items.title: Heated Rivalry, The Housemaid and Fourth Wing: TikTok launches BookTok bestseller list
items.summary: The inaugural top 20 is entirely made up of female authors, with Chloe Walsh appearing most frequently.

items.title: Video shows correspondents' dinner suspect charge checkpoint
items.summary: Surveillance video released by Donald Trump shows the suspect running past metal detectors as security agents draw their guns.

items.title: 'You just didn't know': Gary O'Donoghue describes confusion and fear at correspondents' dinner
items.summary: Police say a man charged security outside of the dining room and was armed with multiple weapons.

items.title: Watch: How one orangutan braved new bridge to unite his split community
items.summary: The forest where the Sumatran orangutans live has been split by a road.

items.title: Hope in Gaza as 300 couples get married at one time
items.summary: The 300 couples were selected in a draw of nearly 2,000 people to be a part of the joint celebration.

items.title: Epstein survivor: Meeting the King would be a 'grand step'
items.summary: A Buckingham Palace source confirmed the Royals will not meet with survivors of sex offender Jeffrey Epstein during their state visit to the US.
